metadata.title: MCMIS Crash File Data Dictionary
metadata.source: FMCSA - Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration

data_elements.display_name: Date & Time Record Was Last Updated
data_elements.definition: Date and time the record was last updated.
A unique identifying number assigned to a crash record.
The state that reported the crash. See State Abbreviations.

data_elements.format: State Abbreviation + 10 characters. No blank spaces.
data_elements.definition: The unique report number designated by the state. Starts with two-letter state code followed by 10 characters.
The date on which the crash occurred.
The military time at which the crash occurred.
Identifies vehicles in a multiple vehicle crash. Value is 1 for single CMV, incremented by 1 for each additional vehicle.
The number assigned by MCMIS to a census record (USDOT number). Each motor carrier should have only one active census number. Numbers are issued sequentially.
Processing status of record.
Date the Status Code is set to C, N, H, or I.
The street address or highway number where the crash occurred.
The 5-digit code for the municipality (city or township) in which the crash occurred as implemented by FIPS PUB 55-3. Can be blank.
The name of the municipality (city or township) in which the crash occurred.
The state/district in which the crash occurred. See State Abbreviations.
The 3-digit Worldwide Geographical Code for the county in which the crash occurred.
Indication of whether the vehicle involved in the crash was a truck or bus.
The degree of trafficway division at the place of the crash.
The degree that access to abutting land is controlled by public authority.
The condition that affected traction on the road surface at the time and location of the crash.

values.7: Sand, Mud, Dirt, Oil or Gravel

data_elements.definition: The cargo body type of the motor vehicle.

values.2: Bus (seats more than 15 people, including driver)

data_elements.definition: Weight rating of the power unit of the vehicle.

values.1: Less than or equal to 10,000 pounds

data_elements.definition: The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) of the motor vehicle. A unique combination of alphanumeric characters formulated by the manufacturer.
The numeric, alphanumeric, or alphabetic character exactly as displayed on the plate or tag affixed to the motor vehicle.
The state/district issuing the license plate of the motor vehicle. See State Abbreviations.
Indication of whether the motor carrier had a hazardous materials placard.
The predominant weather condition at the time and place of the crash.

values.6: Blowing Sand, Soil, Dirt, or Snow

data_elements.definition: The configuration of the motor vehicle.

values.1: Passenger Car (Only if Vehicle displays HM Placard)
values.2: Light Truck (Only if Vehicle displays HM Placard)
values.99: Unknown Heavy Truck > 10000 lbs, Cannot Classify

data_elements.definition: Light condition at the time and place of the crash.
Indicates whether or not Hazardous Material was released as a result of the crash.
The agency responsible for investigating and reporting the crash.
Total number of vehicles or vehicle combinations involved in the crash. Includes all trucks, buses, and other vehicles (cars, pedalcycles, etc.).
Number of persons killed inside or outside a vehicle at the scene of the crash.
Number of persons injured inside or outside a vehicle at the scene of the crash who were transported to a medical facility for immediate medical attention.
Indication of whether one or more motor vehicles were disabled and transported away from the scene by a tow truck or another vehicle.

Indication of whether the crash was Federally Recordable. A recordable crash means at least one person dies, or at least one person requires immediate medical treatment away from scene, or a vehicle is towed away.
Indication of whether the crash was State Recordable.
SafetyNet version number used for the crash record.
SafetyNet sequence identifier for the crash record.
Transaction code indicating whether the record was added or changed.
Date and time of the transaction.

First character of the DOT number sent by states in the Crash Upload (version 10 and before). If the DOT number is valid this will be a zero. States sometimes fill in F for federal number or S for state number if unable to assign a DOT number.
The rest of the uploaded DOT number. Combined with UPLOAD_FIRST_BYTE, this field is used in the Carrier Match Process.
Identifies if a DOT number search was conducted. Y = Yes, or blank.
Date and time the record was uploaded.
Date and time the record was added.
Unique identifier for the crash carrier record.
Name of the carrier involved in the crash.
Street address of the carrier.
City of the carrier address.
City code of the carrier address. Can be blank.
State of the carrier address. See State Abbreviations.
Zip code of the carrier address.
Mexican Colonia of the carrier address.
Docket number associated with the carrier.
Indicates whether the carrier operates interstate.
Flag indicating no census number available.
State-assigned census number for the carrier.
State that issued the state census number. See State Abbreviations.
Contains in order the Crash Event Sequence Number, Crash Event ID, and Crash Event Description separated by colons (:). Multiple Crash Events are concatenated separated by semi colons (;).

event_values.5: Non collision cargo loss or shift
event_values.9: Non collision equipment failure (brake failure, blown tires, etc.)
event_values.13: Collision involving motor vehicle in transport
event_values.19: Collision with work zone maintenance equipment

glossary.BUS: Any motor vehicle designed, constructed, and used for the commercial transportation of 15 or more passengers, including the driver.
glossary.COMMERCIAL_VEHICLE: A motor vehicle, usually a truck or bus, which transports freight or passengers for compensation.
glossary.CVSA: Commercial Vehicle Safety Alliance - an organization of states and provinces in the US, Canada, and Mexico dedicated to improving the uniformity of commercial motor vehicle safety enforcement.
glossary.DEFECT_GROUP: The group to which a given violation is attributed: vehicle, driver, or hazardous materials.
glossary.DOUBLE: A commercial motor vehicle consisting of a truck-tractor and two detachable trailers.
glossary.DRIVER_ONLY_INSPECTION: Examines only driver-related aspects (Level III inspection).
glossary.DRIVER_VIOLATION: A violation pertaining to the driver of the commercial vehicle.
glossary.FACILITY_TYPE: The type of facility - fixed or roadside - where the inspection was conducted.
glossary.FMCSA: Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration - the administration within USDOT responsible for monitoring and developing safety standards for commercial motor vehicles.
glossary.FMCSR: Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations - regulations governing safe operation of commercial vehicles in interstate commerce (Title 49 CFR, Subtitle B, Chapter III).
glossary.FIXED_FACILITY: A state commercial vehicle scale facility or other permanent site used to conduct inspections.
glossary.FLEET_SIZE: Total number of power units (truck-tractors and straight trucks) owned or operated by a motor carrier.
glossary.FOR_HIRE_CARRIER: A commercial motor carrier whose primary business is transportation of property or passengers by motor vehicle for compensation.
glossary.FULL_INSPECTION: Level I inspection - the most comprehensive, involving extensive vehicle checks including under-vehicle brake measurement and hours-of-service logs.
glossary.HAZARDOUS_MATERIALS: Materials, substances, or wastes which may be toxic, harmful, or fatal if accidentally exposed to humans, animals, or the environment.
glossary.HMR: Hazardous Materials Regulations - federal regulations governing commercial transportation of hazardous materials (Title 49 CFR, Subtitle B, Chapter I).
glossary.INTERSTATE_CARRIER: A carrier who sometimes or always operates in interstate or foreign commerce, including carriers of hazardous materials.
glossary.INTRASTATE_CARRIER: A carrier who operates solely in intrastate commerce and never transports hazardous materials.
glossary.MCMIS: Motor Carrier Management Information System - the computerized system operated by FMCSA containing comprehensive safety data on interstate and intrastate commercial carriers.
glossary.MCSAP: Motor Carrier Safety Assistance Program - a Federal program providing funds to US states and territories for commercial motor vehicle safety activities.
glossary.OOS_VIOLATIONS: Out-of-Service violations requiring a commercial vehicle or driver be taken off the road until the violation is resolved.
glossary.POTENTIAL_RESOLUTION: Crash record that does not match a regulated entity within FMCSA registration system.
glossary.PRIVATE_CARRIER: A commercial motor carrier for which private highway transportation activities are incidental to its primary business.
glossary.SAFETYNET: A state-based information system used to store and process commercial carrier safety information.
glossary.SINGLE: A commercial motor vehicle consisting of a truck-tractor and a detachable trailer.
glossary.STRAIGHT_TRUCK: A commercial motor vehicle in which the power unit and cargo box are non-detachable.
glossary.TRIPLE: A commercial motor vehicle consisting of a truck-tractor and three detachable trailers.
glossary.TRUCK_TRACTOR: A self-propelled motor vehicle designed and primarily used to draw other vehicles.
glossary.USDOT_NUMBER: An identification number assigned to all interstate commercial carriers regulated by FMCSA.
glossary.VEHICLE_CONFIGURATION: Arrangement of the individual units comprising a commercial vehicle.
glossary.WALK_AROUND_INSPECTION: Level II inspection - follows most Full Inspection procedures except under-vehicle actions.

county_codes_note: Full county code lookup table included below. Structure: state_abbreviation -> [{county_name, code}]
county_codes: See the companion .md file for the complete county code reference table. The full list spans all 50 US states, DC, Puerto Rico, and US Virgin Islands with FIPS-based county codes.
